title: Reason Code Registry
description: Stable identifiers for every state-transition denial, mutation-authority denial/outcome, schema-validation failure, ontology-binding denial, memory-retention violation, and migration-reconciliation failure the successor's contracts reference by `reasonCode`. Every code is `code`/`owner`/`meaning`/`retryable`, matching execution-plan.md section 6.3's 'Every transition must be an explicit decision with stable reason codes' and section 6.4's 'stable denial and outcome reason codes'. Codes are never renumbered or reused; a superseded code is retired, not deleted (see tests/contracts/reason-code-registry.test.ts for the uniqueness + stability test this registry must pass). Runtime-neutral. Real content P330.

codes.meaning: A state-advancing transition attempted to skip an intermediate state in the FDE/SIC/DTC or Altitude-2 operation state machine (execution-plan.md section 6.3), e.g. DTC_PROPOSED -> COMMITTED directly.
A state-advancing transition was attempted without independently verified user-decision evidence (not tool success, not a free-text reference).
The SIC or DTC fingerprint presented at a transition no longer matches the currently-approved body.
The mutation-authority envelope's expiresAt has passed before the commit gate resolved it.
The envelope's one-time or bounded-use nonce has already been consumed by a prior commit attempt.
The envelope's sicFingerprint or dtcFingerprint does not match the currently-approved SIC/DTC body at resolution time.
submissionCriteriaResult.passed was false at resolution time (closes P220 section 8.4's vacuous-when-omitted gap: this field is mandatory, never optional).
permissionDecisionRef resolved to a deny at commit-gate resolution time.
expectedPriorState (the concurrency token) no longer matches the target's actual prior state at commit time.
A target identity fell outside the envelope's declared allowedAction/writeScope.
The dry-run fingerprint captured at issuance no longer matches the actual write plan at commit time.
The single commit gate resolved the envelope and the write completed. Terminal success outcome.
A payload failed validation against its bound contracts/*.contract.json schema (missing or malformed load-bearing field).
The payload's schemaVersion is not recognized by the current successor build.
consumerProjectId or consumerOntologyId on an ontology-binding does not resolve to a registered consumer project.
The attempted Altitude-2 operation exceeds the ontology-binding's bindingScope (e.g. a commit attempted under a read-only or proposal-only binding).
An attempted read or write on a memory item conflicts with its declared retention.policy (e.g. writing to an archived item outside the archive-before-remove path).
reconciliation.actualHash does not equal reconciliation.expectedHash for a migration manifest.
reconciliation.actualCount does not equal reconciliation.expectedCount for a migration manifest.
A checkpoint reached status:"failed" and rollback.available must be exercised before the migration can proceed.

A staged primitive's readiness evaluation for one DATA/LOGIC/ACTION evidence class found no matching evidence item in that class's own collection; readiness for that class can never be satisfied by evidence recorded under another class (execution-plan.md section 9 row P420).
An evidence item's own declared evidenceClass does not match the DATA/LOGIC/ACTION collection it was submitted under (e.g. a LOGIC-declared item placed in the DATA evidence collection).

Construction validation (CONSTRUCTION_STAGED -> VALIDATED) was attempted while the staged primitive's DATA/LOGIC/ACTION readiness was not confirmed for all three evidence classes; this is never inferred or auto-promoted.

A staged primitive's kind is not one of the seven registered product-primitive kinds (Object Type, Property, Shared Property Type, Link, Interface, Action, Function). ADR-003's ControlPlaneNodeKind surfaces are never a valid value here.

A ControlPlaneNodeKind catalog entry's kind matches one of the seven registered product-primitive kinds (ADR-001/ADR-003 boundary): a control-plane node may never be registered as, or collide with, a product Ontology primitive, and vice versa.
